name: Dr Pepper BBQ Sauce
directions: Juice the lemon, you should get 4 tablespoons.
In a saucepan add all ingredients, bring to a boil.
Lower to a simmer and cook 10-15 minutes until thickened.
You can add more vinegar/steak sauce/ hot sauce/ if you need it, to taste.

similar.name: Mom's Tangy Mustard Sauce for Cocktail Franks

name: Yoghurty Garlicy Herby Dipping Sauce
directions: Combine ingredients in a bowl.

name: Cocoa Sauce from Mexico
directions: In small saucepan, mix cocoa, sugar and cinnamon.
Stir in water and bring to a boil over medium heat.
Cook 5 minutes, stirring constantly.
Remove from heat and add cream and kahlua.
Mix well.
Cool sauce completely at room temperature.
Transfer to a jar with a tight-fitting lid.
Sauce keeps in refridgerator for one month.

similar.name: Mom's Tangy Mustard Sauce for Cocktail Franks

name: Cajun Red Dipping Sauce
directions: Combine all ingredients.
Cover and chill overnight.

similar.name: Mom's Tangy Mustard Sauce for Cocktail Franks

name: Yogurt Cilantro Sauce for Fish
directions: Puree 1/2 cup yogurt, cilantro, ginger, garlic, jalapenos, lime juice, and salt in a food processor or blender.
Taste and modify taste by adding additional yogurt and/or jalapenos as desired.
Cover.
Refrigerate up to 3 days.
Serve over grilled or broiled or pan grilled fish.

name: Carrot Sauce
directions: Dissolve the potato starch in the broth.
Mix the potato starch mixture with the melted margarine.
Stir constantly over low heat until mixture is smooth and thick.
Add carrots and ketchup.
Continue to cook over low heat for 5 minutes.
Add salt and pepper to taste.

similar.name: Mom's Tangy Mustard Sauce for Cocktail Franks

name: Mom's Tangy Mustard Sauce for Cocktail Franks
directions: Melt margarine in a medium saucepan and set aside.
In a small bowl, using a mixer, beat eggs, sugars, mustard and paprika together.
Add vinegar and beat again.
Pour this into the saucepan with the melted, cooled, margarine.
Cook over low heat, stirring  just until thickened; about 4-5 minutes.

name: Yaya's Salsa Blanca (White Sauce)
directions: Dissolve flour in 1/2 cup of milk (or soymilk).  Set aside.
Warm the remaining milk in a saucepan, over med heat. Do not let it boil.
Add butter (or margarine), salt, pepper and nutmeg.  Do not be afraid to use the nutmeg, it makes the sauce!
Add the dissolved flour/milk mixture.  Whisk over med heat until it thickens.
For an even richer sauce, remove from stove and quickly whisk in an egg yolk.
